questions.prompt: Why run brand Search exact-match as a separate campaign when PMax is also running?

choices.text: Google's documented rule: exact-match Search keywords take priority over PMax for matching that query. Brand Search exact-match catches brand queries before PMax does — with the granular control, reporting visibility, and tight bid management you want for brand. Without it, PMax absorbs brand queries with inferior handling: blended audience signals, less precise matching, and no isolation for measuring brand-specific performance. Always run brand Search exact-match in its own campaign with aggressive bid willingness.

questions.explanation: Exact-match Search wins priority over PMax. Always isolate brand Search.

questions.prompt: Mature performance account spending $50k/mo on Search and PMax. New-customer growth has plateaued. What's missing?

choices.text: Top-of-funnel investment. Search and PMax both optimize for users with existing intent — they harvest demand that already exists. Without a discovery-stage channel feeding the consideration funnel, you eventually saturate the existing-demand pool and new-customer growth flatlines. Allocate 10-20% of paid budget to Demand Gen (visual ads on YouTube Shorts, Discover, Gmail) to drive awareness and consideration. Measure with brand-search lift, direct traffic increases, and longer-window attribution — short-window CPA will look worse but the downstream lift is real.

questions.explanation: Add Demand Gen for top-of-funnel discovery. Search/PMax can't grow without it.
